---
title: "Singapore's Ridership Recovered - But Not in the Same Shape"
subtitle: "Bus, MRT and LRT recovery relative to 2019"
author: "Team 12"
date: 2026-07-28
date-format: "D MMMM YYYY"
format:
  revealjs:
    self-contained: true
    embed-resources: true
    theme: [default, presentation.scss]
    width: 1600
    height: 900
    margin: 0.055
    min-scale: 0.2
    max-scale: 2.0
    slide-number: true
    preview-links: false
    incremental: false
    transition: fade
    footer: "AAI1001 - Data Engineering and Visualization | Team 12"
    html-table-processing: none
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

## Team 12 {.unnumbered .balanced}

| Team member | Student ID |
|---|---:|
| Thaw Zin Htun | 2502712 |
| Siyona Shailesh Naik | 2502714 |
| Wilson Teng Hao Feng | 2503002 |
| Troy Ang | 2503451 |
| Zayar | 2502688 |

::: {.callout}
All five members will contribute to the oral presentation and audience Q&A.
:::

::: {.notes}
Suggested speaker: Thaw Zin Htun. Cover slides 1-5 in about 2 minutes 30 seconds, ending with the original chart.
:::

## The total returned; the mix changed {.unnumbered .balanced}

::: {.callout}
By 2025, average daily public transport ridership had recovered to **98.0% of 2019**, but MRT was above its pre-pandemic level while bus remained below it.
:::

::: {.metric-row}
::: {.metric}
**103.1**
MRT recovery index  
2019 = 100
:::
::: {.metric}
**93.7**
Bus recovery index  
2019 = 100
:::
::: {.metric}
**100.5**
LRT recovery index  
2019 = 100
:::
:::

Our question: **How did recovery differ by mode, and what changed beneath the near-recovered total?**

<p class="source-note">Source: Land Transport Authority, Average Public Transport Ridership By Year (2015-2025). Values are average daily rides.</p>

## The project moves from critique to evidence {.balanced}

| Project task | What this presentation demonstrates |
|---|---|
| Original visualization | A quantitative Straits Times chart and its public-transport recovery story |
| Critical analysis | Specific strengths and four evidence-based weaknesses |
| Improvement plan | One implemented response for each weakness |
| Data engineering | Traceable raw data, cleaning, transformations, validation and processed output |
| Visualization | One interactive chart with three metric views and complete labelling |
| Data analysis | Recovery gaps, mode-share shifts and year-on-year momentum |

::: {.callout}
The central question remains consistent across every stage: **did all transport modes recover in the same way?**
:::

## The original chart tells the broad recovery story {.balanced}

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="58%"}
![Original Straits Times visualization of average daily public transport rides, 2019-2025.](ChosenVisualization.png){width="100%"}
:::
::: {.column width="42%"}

- One row per year
- Stacked segments for bus, MRT and LRT
- Direct totals and bus/MRT values
- Stronger colour for the latest year

**Useful:** total and composition appear together.  
**Limitation:** the recovery comparison is present, but not directly encoded.
:::
::::

<p class="source-note">Sources: <a href="https://www.straitstimes.com/singapore/transport/more-people-used-mrt-in-2025-as-lrt-ridership-falls-bus-numbers-still-below-pre-covid-levels" target="_blank">The Straits Times article and graphic</a>; underlying values verified against the <a href="https://www.lta.gov.sg/content/dam/ltagov/who_we_are/statistics_and_publications/statistics/pdf/PT_Ridership_Yearly_2015-2025.pdf" target="_blank">LTA yearly ridership table</a>.</p>

## Four strengths are worth preserving {.balanced}

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

### S1. Total and mode split
Readers can see overall recovery and each mode's contribution in one view.

### S2. Chronological rows
The 2020 fall and subsequent recovery are easy to follow.

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

### S3. Stable mode colours
Bus, MRT and LRT remain recognisable across years.

### S4. Direct context
The title, unit, legend, totals and source establish what is measured.

:::
::::

::: {.callout}
The redesign should improve comparison without losing the original chart's compact overview and clear mode identity.
:::

::: {.notes}
Suggested speaker: Siyona Shailesh Naik. Cover slides 6-10 in about 2 minutes 45 seconds, ending with the raw-data structure.
:::

## Four weaknesses explain the redesign {.balanced}

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

### W1. Baseline is under-emphasised
2019 is not marked as the baseline; faded older rows and the apparent default selection weaken the central comparison.

### W2. Modes lack a common start
Only bus starts at a common baseline; the thin, unlabelled LRT segment is especially difficult to compare.

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

### W3. Key changes are not calculated
Recovery gaps and year-on-year changes require readers to perform the arithmetic themselves.

### W4. Mode-share shift is hidden
Stacked totals conceal the shift from bus toward MRT.

:::
::::

<p class="source-note">Marker feedback applied: former W1/W6 and W2/W3 were merged; the list was reduced from six weaknesses to four.</p>

## Each weakness has one justified response {.balanced .compact}

| Weakness | Implemented change | Why it improves the story |
|---|---|---|
| **W1** | Recovery index with a visible **2019 = 100** reference and equal emphasis on load | Makes above/below-baseline recovery immediate and removes a misleading default selection |
| **W2** | Separate mode lines, direct endpoint labels and exact LRT tooltip values | Gives every mode a common baseline and keeps small LRT changes readable |
| **W3** | Tooltips show rides, recovery, gap and year-on-year change | Removes manual arithmetic and connects each point to interpretable evidence |
| **W4** | The same chart switches among recovery, absolute rides and mode share | Answers three related questions without creating a dashboard |

::: {.callout}
The redesign remains **one interactive chart**, consistent with the project brief. We retained the planned recovery-index view and metric control, but omitted the optional total line because it reduced mode-level readability; the total remains available in the surrounding evidence.
:::

## We use one official yearly dataset {.balanced}

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}

**Primary sources**  
[LTA DataMall yearly CSV](https://datamall.lta.gov.sg/content/dam/datamall/datasets/Facts_Figures/Public%20Transport/yearly_ave_daily_pt_ridership.csv){target="_blank"} and the [LTA yearly ridership table](https://www.lta.gov.sg/content/dam/ltagov/who_we_are/statistics_and_publications/statistics/pdf/PT_Ridership_Yearly_2015-2025.pdf){target="_blank"}.

**Selected scope**  
2019-2025; Public Bus, MRT and LRT; average rides per day.

**Why it fits**  
The values reproduce the news graphic's scope and provide all years and modes needed for the comparison.

:::
::: {.column width="45%"}

| Check | Result |
|---|---:|
| Expected records | 21 |
| Missing year-mode pairs | 0 |
| Duplicate year-mode pairs | 0 |
| Negative ridership values | 0 |
| 2025 total | 7.540M |

:::
::::

<p class="source-note">DataMall last update: April 2026; retrieved 16 July 2026. Local raw file: <code>data/raw/yearly_ave_daily_pt_ridership.csv</code>.</p>

## The raw data is already in a useful long format {.balanced}

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="48%"}

| Raw field | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `year` | Calendar year |
| `mode` | Public Bus, MRT or LRT |
| `ridership` | Full average daily ride count |

One row represents one **year-mode observation**.

:::
::: {.column width="52%"}

| year | mode | ridership |
|---:|---|---:|
| 2019 | Public Bus | 4,099,000 |
| 2019 | MRT | 3,384,000 |
| 2019 | LRT | 208,000 |
| 2025 | Public Bus | 3,841,000 |
| 2025 | MRT | 3,490,000 |
| 2025 | LRT | 209,000 |

:::
::::

<p class="source-note">The official yearly dataset is used for calculations; values are not digitised from the news image.</p>

## Data quality is mainly an interpretation problem {.balanced .compact}

| Risk | Why it matters | Treatment |
|---|---|---|
| Unit mismatch | CSV stores full counts; article and PDF show thousands | Preserve full counts; derive thousands and millions only for display |
| Mode naming | LTA uses “Public Bus”; the news chart uses “Bus” | Standardise to `Bus` after retaining the source label |
| Measure meaning | Values are average daily rides, not annual totals | State the measure in titles, labels and tooltips |
| LRT scale | Small absolute values make tiny changes easy to overstate | Show exact values and interpret the 1,000-ride decline cautiously |
| Annual authority | A naive monthly average may not reproduce the yearly figure | Use the official yearly dataset as the plotted source |

::: {.callout}
No values are missing; the main quality safeguard is preserving units, meaning and traceability.
:::

::: {.notes}
Suggested speaker: Wilson Teng Hao Feng. Cover slides 11-15 in about 3 minutes, ending with the reproducibility chain.
:::

## The pipeline is implemented from raw to chart-ready {.balanced}

::: {.pipeline}
<div><strong>1. Ingest</strong><br>Read the LTA yearly CSV with explicit year, mode and ridership fields.</div>
<div><strong>2. Filter</strong><br>Keep 2019-2025 and the three public transport modes.</div>
<div><strong>3. Standardise</strong><br>Rename Public Bus to Bus and parse integer ridership values.</div>
<div><strong>4. Derive</strong><br>Calculate totals, recovery index, gaps, YoY change and mode share.</div>
<div><strong>5. Validate</strong><br>Assert complete year-mode coverage before writing 21 chart-ready rows.</div>
:::

```text
recovery_index = ridership / ridership_2019 x 100
mode_share_pct = mode_ridership / yearly_total x 100
year_on_year_pct = (ridership / prior_year_ridership - 1) x 100
```

<p class="source-note">Implemented in <code>scripts/build_ridership_metrics.R</code>; output: <code>data/processed/ridership_metrics_2019_2025.csv</code>.</p>

## Derived measures connect engineering to the story {.balanced .compact}

| Measure | Calculation | Question answered |
|---|---|---|
| Yearly total | Sum Bus, MRT and LRT within each year | How close is the overall system to 2019? |
| Recovery index | `ridership / 2019 ridership x 100` | Which modes exceeded the baseline? |
| Gap from 2019 | `ridership - 2019 ridership` | How many daily rides remain above or below 2019? |
| Mode share | `mode ridership / yearly total x 100` | Did the transport mix change? |
| Year-on-year change | `(current / previous - 1) x 100` | When was recovery fastest? |

::: {.callout}
The final chart-ready dataset has **nine fields**: year, mode, ridership, display units, recovery index, gap, change from 2019, year-on-year change and mode share. Every displayed insight is derived from these fields.
:::

## Validation checks run before chart creation {.balanced}

::: {.metric-row}
::: {.metric}
**21 / 21**
Expected year-mode records present
:::
::: {.metric}
**0**
Missing or duplicate pairs
:::
::: {.metric}
**0**
Negative ridership values
:::
:::

Additional checks confirm the published 2025 values:

- Bus: **3,841,000** average daily rides
- MRT: **3,490,000** average daily rides
- LRT: **209,000** average daily rides
- Combined total: **7,540,000**

<p class="source-note">The script stops with an error if the expected 2019-2025 year-mode coverage is incomplete.</p>

## The implementation is reproducible and portable {.balanced}

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

**Inputs**

- Unchanged raw yearly CSV
- Documented LTA source links
- Original chart image for critique only

**Transformation**

- `scripts/build_ridership_metrics.R`
- Base R; runnable through RStudio or `Rscript`
- No hidden notebook state or manual calculations

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

**Outputs**

- 21-row processed metrics CSV
- Generated JavaScript chart-data resource
- Self-contained RevealJS HTML
- Resource ZIP containing source, data, styling and script

**Rerun path**

`raw CSV -> R script -> processed CSV -> Quarto render`

:::
::::

## The improved chart makes recovery directly comparable {.balanced}

::: {.chart-shell}
<div class="chart-controls" aria-label="Chart metric">
  <button type="button" data-metric="recovery" class="active">Recovery index</button>
  <button type="button" data-metric="absolute">Absolute rides</button>
  <button type="button" data-metric="share">Mode share</button>
</div>
<svg id="ridership-chart" viewBox="0 0 1450 500" role="img" aria-label="Interactive chart of public transport ridership by mode from 2019 to 2025"></svg>
<div class="chart-note">Direct labels identify each mode. Hover or focus a point for exact values. Source: <a href="https://www.lta.gov.sg/content/dam/ltagov/who_we_are/statistics_and_publications/statistics/pdf/PT_Ridership_Yearly_2015-2025.pdf" target="_blank">Land Transport Authority</a>.</div>
:::
<div id="chart-tooltip" class="chart-tooltip"></div>

```{=html}
<script src="data/processed/ridership_chart_data.js"></script>
<script>
(() => {
  const rows = (window.RIDERSHIP_CHART_DATA || []).map(d => ({
    year: Number(d.year),
    mode: d.mode,
    ridership: Number(d.ridership),
    recovery: Number(d.recovery_index),
    absolute: Number(d.ridership) / 1000000,
    share: Number(d.mode_share_pct),
    gap: Number(d.gap_from_2019),
    yoy: d.year_on_year_pct === null ? null : Number(d.year_on_year_pct)
  }));
  const years = [...new Set(rows.map(d => d.year))];
  const modes = ['Bus','MRT','LRT'];
  const colors = {Bus:'#0072B2', MRT:'#D55E00', LRT:'#009E73'};
  const labels = {
    recovery: ['Recovery index (2019 = 100)', d => d.recovery, v => v.toFixed(1)],
    absolute: ['Average daily rides (millions)', d => d.absolute, v => v.toFixed(1)+'M'],
    share: ['Share of yearly total (%)', d => d.share, v => v.toFixed(1)+'%']
  };
  const domains = {
    recovery: [50, 110],
    absolute: [0, 4.5],
    share: [0, 60]
  };
  const tickValues = {
    recovery: [50, 60, 70, 80, 90, 100, 110],
    absolute: [0, 1, 2, 3, 4],
    share: [0, 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60]
  };
  const tickFormat = {
    recovery: v => String(v),
    absolute: v => v === 0 ? '0' : v + 'M',
    share: v => v + '%'
  };
  let metric = 'recovery';
  const svg = document.getElementById('ridership-chart');
  const tip = document.getElementById('chart-tooltip');
  if (!svg || !tip) return;
  if (!rows.length) {
    svg.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Chart data failed to load');
    svg.textContent = 'Chart data failed to load. Re-run the R build script and render the presentation.';
    return;
  }
  const NS='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg', W=1450, H=500, m={l:105,r:135,t:70,b:58};
  const node=(name,attrs={},text='')=>{ const n=document.createElementNS(NS,name); Object.entries(attrs).forEach(([k,v])=>n.setAttribute(k,v)); if(text)n.textContent=text; return n; };
  function render(){
    svg.replaceChildren();
    const get=labels[metric][1];
    const [min,max]=domains[metric];
    const x=y=>m.l+(y-2019)/(2025-2019)*(W-m.l-m.r);
    const yy=v=>H-m.b-(v-min)/(max-min)*(H-m.t-m.b);
    tickValues[metric].forEach(val=>{
      const py=yy(val);
      svg.append(node('line',{x1:m.l,x2:W-m.r,y1:py,y2:py,stroke:'#dfe8e6','stroke-width':1}));
      svg.append(node('text',{x:m.l-14,y:py+6,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':20},tickFormat[metric](val)));
    });
    if(metric==='recovery'){
      const py=yy(100); svg.append(node('line',{x1:m.l,x2:W-m.r,y1:py,y2:py,stroke:'#516b70','stroke-width':2,'stroke-dasharray':'8 7'}));
      svg.append(node('text',{x:m.l+18,y:py-13,'text-anchor':'start','font-size':20,'font-weight':700},'2019 baseline = 100'));
    }
    years.forEach(y=>{ const px=x(y); svg.append(node('text',{x:px,y:H-30,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':20},String(y))); });
    svg.append(node('text',{x:(m.l+W-m.r)/2,y:H-5,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':20,'font-weight':700},'Year'));
    svg.append(node('text',{x:m.l,y:30,'font-size':24,'font-weight':700},labels[metric][0]));
    modes.forEach(mode=>{
      const series=rows.filter(d=>d.mode===mode), points=series.map(d=>`${x(d.year)},${yy(get(d))}`).join(' ');
      svg.append(node('polyline',{points,fill:'none',stroke:colors[mode],'stroke-width':5,'stroke-linejoin':'round','stroke-linecap':'round'}));
      series.forEach(d=>{
        const c=node('circle',{cx:x(d.year),cy:yy(get(d)),r:7,fill:colors[mode],stroke:'white','stroke-width':3,tabindex:0,'aria-label':`${d.mode} ${d.year}`});
        const show=e=>{
          const gapText=(d.gap>=0?'+':'')+(d.gap/1000).toFixed(0)+'k';
          const gapClass=d.gap>0?'positive':d.gap<0?'negative':'';
          const yoyText=d.yoy===null?'baseline':(d.yoy>=0?'+':'')+d.yoy.toFixed(1)+'%';
          const yoyClass=d.yoy===null?'':d.yoy>=0?'positive':'negative';
          tip.innerHTML=`<strong>${d.mode}, ${d.year}</strong><br>${(d.ridership/1000000).toFixed(3)}M rides/day<br>Recovery: ${d.recovery.toFixed(1)}<br>Gap from 2019: <span class="${gapClass}">${gapText}</span><br>YoY: <span class="${yoyClass}">${yoyText}</span><br>Mode share: ${d.share.toFixed(1)}%`;
          tip.style.opacity=1; const p=e.touches?e.touches[0]:e; tip.style.left=((p.clientX||700)+15)+'px'; tip.style.top=((p.clientY||300)+15)+'px';
        };
        c.addEventListener('mousemove',show); c.addEventListener('focus',()=>show({clientX:700,clientY:300})); c.addEventListener('mouseleave',()=>tip.style.opacity=0); c.addEventListener('blur',()=>tip.style.opacity=0); svg.append(c);
      });
      const end=series.at(-1);
      const labelNudge=metric==='recovery'?(mode==='MRT'?-8:mode==='LRT'?8:0):0;
      svg.append(node('text',{x:x(2025)+16,y:yy(get(end))+7+labelNudge,'font-size':21,'font-weight':700,fill:colors[mode]},`${mode} ${labels[metric][2](get(end))}`));
    });
  }
  document.querySelectorAll('.chart-controls button').forEach(btn=>btn.addEventListener('click',()=>{ metric=btn.dataset.metric; document.querySelectorAll('.chart-controls button').forEach(b=>b.classList.toggle('active',b===btn)); render(); }));
  render();
})();
</script>
```

::: {.notes}
Suggested speaker: Troy Ang. Cover slides 16-20 in about 3 minutes 15 seconds. Demonstrate all three metric buttons, then return to Recovery index before continuing.
:::

## Every redesign choice resolves a critique {.balanced .design-choices}

```{=html}
<div class="resolution-list">
  <div class="resolution-item">
    <div class="resolution-code">W1</div>
    <div><h3>Common 2019 baseline</h3><p>All modes begin at 100, with no mode pre-highlighted. Above- and below-baseline recovery is immediately comparable.</p></div>
  </div>
  <div class="resolution-item">
    <div class="resolution-code">W2</div>
    <div><h3>Equal visibility by mode</h3><p>Separate indexed lines keep MRT and LRT readable instead of compressing them within stacked totals.</p></div>
  </div>
  <div class="resolution-item">
    <div class="resolution-code">W3</div>
    <div><h3>Exact values on demand</h3><p>Keyboard-accessible tooltips provide rides, recovery, the 2019 gap, year-on-year change and mode share.</p></div>
  </div>
  <div class="resolution-item">
    <div class="resolution-code">W4</div>
    <div><h3>One chart, three views</h3><p>The control reveals recovery, absolute scale and composition without turning the result into a dashboard.</p></div>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Three analyses answer three distinct questions {.balanced}

| Analysis | Method | Evidence produced |
|---|---|---|
| **A1. Recovery gap** | Compare each mode's 2025 value with its fixed 2019 baseline | Index, absolute gap and percentage change |
| **A2. Mode-share shift** | Divide each mode by the yearly three-mode total | 2019 versus 2025 percentage-point change |
| **A3. Recovery momentum** | Compare each year with the preceding year by mode | Largest increases and any later setbacks |

::: {.callout}
These are descriptive methods suited to seven yearly observations per mode; no causal model is claimed.
:::

## MRT exceeded 2019 while bus remained below {.balanced}

::: {.metric-row}
::: {.metric}
<strong class="positive">+106k</strong>
MRT daily rides versus 2019  
<strong class="positive">+3.1%</strong>
:::
::: {.metric}
<strong class="negative">-258k</strong>
Bus daily rides versus 2019  
<strong class="negative">-6.3%</strong>
:::
::: {.metric}
<strong class="positive">+1k</strong>
LRT daily rides versus 2019  
<strong class="positive">+0.5%</strong>
:::
:::

The modes therefore did not recover uniformly. The 2025 total masks a **shift in where journeys occurred**.

<p class="source-note">Calculated from LTA yearly values: MRT 3.384M to 3.490M; Bus 4.099M to 3.841M; LRT 0.208M to 0.209M.</p>

## The ridership mix shifted from bus toward MRT {.balanced}

| Mode | 2019 share | 2025 share | Change |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Bus | 53.3% | 50.9% | <span class="negative">-2.4 pp</span> |
| MRT | 44.0% | 46.3% | <span class="positive">+2.3 pp</span> |
| LRT | 2.7% | 2.8% | <span class="positive">+0.1 pp</span> |

In 2025, MRT added **78,000** average daily rides year on year (<span class="positive">+2.3%</span>), while bus added only **4,000** (<span class="positive">+0.1%</span>). LRT declined by **1,000** (<span class="negative">-0.5%</span>).

::: {.callout}
The near-recovered total is therefore not a return to the 2019 transport mix.
:::

<p class="source-note">Source: LTA yearly ridership data; percentage-point changes are calculated from unrounded mode shares.</p>

## Recovery momentum peaked in 2022 {.balanced}

| Mode | Largest post-2020 increase | Change |
|---|---:|---:|
| Bus | 2021 to 2022 | <span class="positive">+15.1%</span> |
| MRT | 2021 to 2022 | <span class="positive">+30.7%</span> |
| LRT | 2021 to 2022 | <span class="positive">+21.9%</span> |

By 2025, growth had slowed to <span class="positive">+0.1% for Bus</span>, <span class="positive">+2.3% for MRT</span>, and <span class="negative">-0.5% for LRT</span>.

::: {.callout}
The system recovered most rapidly in 2022, then shifted from broad rebound toward slower, mode-specific change.
:::

<p class="source-note">Source: LTA yearly ridership data; year-on-year percentages are calculated within each transport mode.</p>

::: {.notes}
Suggested speaker: Zayar. Cover slides 21-25 in about 2 minutes 30 seconds, state the conclusion clearly, then invite questions.
:::

## What the data can - and cannot - establish {.balanced}

**Supported by the dataset**

- Recovery relative to 2019 differs by mode.
- MRT's share increased while bus share decreased.
- The 2025 total remained 151,000 rides per day below 2019.

**Not established by this dataset alone**

- Why travellers changed modes.
- Whether remote work, network expansion or service changes caused the shift.
- Individual trip purpose, route, distance or passenger characteristics.

The analysis is descriptive; causal explanations require additional evidence.

## Conclusion: recovery was strong, but uneven {.balanced}

::: {.callout}
Singapore's average daily public transport ridership reached **7.540 million in 2025**, or **98.0% of 2019**. Yet MRT exceeded its 2019 level, bus remained below it, and MRT gained about **2.3 percentage points** of mode share.
:::

The improved visualization makes that conclusion visible immediately by:

1. anchoring every mode to the same 2019 baseline,
2. giving LRT an equally readable series,
3. showing calculated gaps and changes, and
4. revealing mode share in the same chart.

## References {.unnumbered .balanced}

- Land Transport Authority. [**Average Public Transport Ridership By Year (2015 to 2025)**](https://www.lta.gov.sg/content/dam/ltagov/who_we_are/statistics_and_publications/statistics/pdf/PT_Ridership_Yearly_2015-2025.pdf){target="_blank"}. Dataset used in the local raw CSV.
- Land Transport Authority. [**Public Transport Utilisation - Average Public Transport Ridership by Year (CSV)**](https://datamall.lta.gov.sg/content/dam/datamall/datasets/Facts_Figures/Public%20Transport/yearly_ave_daily_pt_ridership.csv){target="_blank"}. Retrieved 16 July 2026.
- Chelvan, V. P. (2026, January 30). [**More rides on MRT in 2025 as LRT use fell; bus ride numbers still below pre-Covid-19 levels.**](https://www.straitstimes.com/singapore/transport/more-people-used-mrt-in-2025-as-lrt-ridership-falls-bus-numbers-still-below-pre-covid-levels){target="_blank"} *The Straits Times*.
- Wong, B. (2011). [**Points of view: Color blindness.**](https://doi.org/10.1038/nmeth.1618){target="_blank"} *Nature Methods, 8*(6), 441.
- Munzner, T. (2014). *Visualization Analysis and Design*. CRC Press.

## Thank you {.unnumbered .balanced .closing}

<div class="qa-title">Questions &amp; discussion</div>

::: {.callout}
**Final takeaway:** Singapore's total ridership nearly recovered by 2025, but the balance shifted toward MRT while Bus remained below its 2019 level.
:::

<p class="source-note">Team 12: Thaw Zin Htun · Siyona Shailesh Naik · Wilson Teng Hao Feng · Troy Ang · Zayar</p>
